response:
The `/decide` process is complete. Summary of what ran: three advisors (lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator) converged on accept-with-a-binding-pilot-gate; the PM drafted ADR 0036 authorizing only a cheap checker-fidelity pilot with the full grid gated on it; independent verification caught a real defect (the ADR's baseline numbers were misattributed to the wrong benchmark run) and red-team caught a second (the false-accept gate as first drafted was statistically unresolvable on the available sample); the PM revised the ADR in place to fix both, grounding the gate in the run's own on-disk numbers and replacing the unresolvable percentage gate with a resolvable, pre-pinned summed-gap budget.

The decision record is written to `docs/decisions/0036-routing-checker-fidelity-pilot.md` (not committed — let me know if you want it committed/PR'd). Full text:

```
---
id: 0036
title: "routing follow-up: checker-fidelity pilot only; grid gated on a pre-registered false-accept-gap budget"
status: accepted
summary: "Issue #109 routing (haiku attempts once, checker accepts-or-escalates, sonnet redoes the whole task uncoached) is a NEW mechanism uncovered by any 0035 trigger. Authorize ONLY a checker-fidelity pilot first — one pre-committed checker vs committed haiku outputs + blind verdicts, zero re-execution. Grid unlocks IFF its summed false-accept frac-met gap <=0.30 over a pinned 6-cell eligible set, derived from the -0.05 floor on THIS run's own 0.350/0.510 baselines; else record the null."
---

# 0036 — routing follow-up: checker-fidelity pilot, grid gated on a false-accept-gap budget

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator — all converged accept-with-binding-pilot-gate. Revised in place pre-merge after the gate found a citation error and an unresolvable-gate defect.
- Context: Issue #109 proposes ROUTING (haiku attempts once; checker accepts-or-escalates; on escalate sonnet redoes the WHOLE task, no coaching), a follow-up to the config 0035 rejected (0035:16). Routing is distinct — quality is bounded by whichever model ships, not by haiku absorbing feedback — uncovered by any 0035 trigger (0035:34-37). Baselines are THIS run's own (benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/results.jsonl, 8 gradient cells; verdict mean_delta -0.16): haiku 0.350, sonnet 0.510, spread 0.160. The 0.315/0.597/0.282 and 25%/6-of-24 figures are the PARALLEL run's (claude/issue-41-8awnsc, via 0035:27 and ISSUE-109.md:10) — a different, larger-spread dataset, the loose prior, NOT this pilot's ground truth.

## Decision
1. **Authorize ONLY the checker-fidelity pilot now** — do NOT fund the grid yet. Exactly ONE checker design, its spec (checklist + accept/escalate rule) committed as a git-timestamped file BEFORE it runs, is executed against the 24 committed haiku-solo outputs (outputs/*.haiku-solo.1.txt) — no re-execution. False-accept is scored ONLY on the pinned eligible set below.
2. **Pinned eligible set + binding gap gate (ADR 0024).** Eligible cells = gradient cells where committed haiku frac-met is below sonnet's by >0.05 (the margin), fixed NOW from results.jsonl: building-skills.1/2/3, engineering-principles.6, optimizing-context.4/5 (gaps 0.167/0.167/0.333/0.133/0.467/0.067). A false-accept = the checker accepts a haiku artifact on an eligible cell. Grid unlocks IFF the summed frac-met gap of all false-accepted eligible cells is **<=0.30**; else record the null append-only (ADR 0019) and STOP. DERIVED: escalate-all routing = sonnet 0.510; each eligible false-accept trades sonnet->haiku, losing its gap over 8 gradient cells, so the -0.05 floor (0.460) allows summed gap <= 0.05x8 = 0.40 at the point estimate; 0.30 holds margin. Any checker accepting optimizing-context.4 (0.467 > 0.40) fails outright — the poka-yoke.
3. **Poka-yoke selection (closes the survivorship break; replaces held-out).** The git-timestamped pre-commit of the ONE checker spec BEFORE the scoring run is the anti-overfit forcing function — the tune/held-out split is dropped (6 eligible cells cannot support a disjoint split). Any variants explored are each committed before their own run and recorded append-only (ADR 0019); adopt binds only to the pre-committed one.
4. **Full-grid adopt bar (only if step 2 unlocks it), pre-registered before any grid cell:** frac-met non-inferior to sonnet-solo (margin -0.05, eval-clustered CI, ADR 0019/0025) AND median cost materially below sonnet-solo. Never difficulty-raise tasks (ADR 0024 D3).

## Justification
Cheap test reusing on-disk artifacts: one checker call over committed text, near-zero spend, interrogating the load-bearing unknown (can a checker escalate the genuinely-worse cells?) BEFORE the grid — ADR 0024's gate-before-spend. At the parallel run's 25% prior on this run's spread, routing's ceiling (0.510 - 0.25x0.160 = 0.470) clears the 0.460 floor by only 0.010 — inside the 8-cell CI, so fidelity must be MEASURED, not assumed.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] Blind frac-met ground truth covers only the 8 gradient cells — verified 2026-07-14: graded/verdicts.jsonl = 72 = 8 gradient x 3 arms x 3 reps (bids-gradient.json); the 16 non-gradient cells carry mechanized scores only, so false-accept is scorable ONLY on the 8 gradient cells (6 eligible). A 24-cell false-accept RATE is not computable here — the 6/24 prior is the parallel run's, not interchangeable.
- [checkable] The <=0.30 gap gate is within the -0.05 floor — verified: point-estimate breach is 0.40, so 0.30 sits 0.0125 frac-met below floor as margin.
- [checkable-doc] Routing is uncovered by 0035's triggers — PM-checked vs 0035:34-37.
- [unverifiable] A deterministic checker can hold summed false-accept gap <=0.30 on a 6-eligible-cell screen — the load-bearing bet, and the sample is small. REOPEN-IF the pre-committed checker returns gap >0.30: record the null, routing does not proceed.

## Rejected alternatives
- Fund the grid now — spends before the checker unknown is measured (ADR 0024).
- Gate on a false-accept PERCENTAGE — on ~6 eligible cells the observable rates (0, 1/6, 2/6...) skip past any percentage line; a summed-gap budget is continuous and resolvable.
- Score over all 24 cells to match the 6/24 prior — no blind ground truth for the 16 non-gradient cells (above); not computable here, and the prior is a different run's.
- Tune/held-out split on the 8 gradient tasks — 6 eligible cells cannot support it; the pre-commit timestamp is the real anti-overfit forcing function.

## Revisit triggers
- Checker gap <=0.30 -> the full-grid adopt bar (Decision 4) fires as pre-registered.
- Gap >0.30 (or no committed checker clears it) -> record the null (ADR 0019); routing closed unless a cheaper/higher-fidelity checker appears.
- Haiku/sonnet frac-met or pricing shifts materially -> the 0.30 budget and eligible set are recomputed.
```

**Bottom line:** proceed with Issue #109's routing experiment, but the only thing authorized right now is a near-zero-spend checker-fidelity pilot scored against already-committed artifacts. The full 8-task grid stays closed until one pre-committed checker holds a summed false-accept gap ≤0.30 over the 6 eligible cells — otherwise the null gets recorded and routing is closed pending a better checker class.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0036-routing-checker-fidelity-pilot.md ---
---
id: 0036
title: "routing follow-up: checker-fidelity pilot only; grid gated on a pre-registered false-accept-gap budget"
status: accepted
summary: "Issue #109 routing (haiku attempts once, checker accepts-or-escalates, sonnet redoes the whole task uncoached) is a NEW mechanism uncovered by any 0035 trigger. Authorize ONLY a checker-fidelity pilot first — one pre-committed checker vs committed haiku outputs + blind verdicts, zero re-execution. Grid unlocks IFF its summed false-accept frac-met gap <=0.30 over a pinned 6-cell eligible set, derived from the -0.05 floor on THIS run's own 0.350/0.510 baselines; else record the null."
---
